epochNote: year 0 = first sunrise (F.A. 1); Y.T. at 1 V.Y.=9.582 sun-years [C]; S.A.=+590; T.A.=+4031; F.o.A.=+7052

events.label: Music of the Ainur; the Valar enter Eä; the Lamps (duration unstated)
The Two Trees first bloom
Awakening of the Elves at Cuiviénen
events.cite: HoME X AAm ('450 such Years before' F.A.)

events.label: The Great March (Anduin reached 1115)
The Eldar come to Aman; Tirion and Alqualondë built
Fëanor devises the Tengwar
Melkor unchained
The Silmarils wrought
Darkening of Valinor; Kinslaying at Alqualondë; the Flight
First Battle of Beleriand: Denethor falls; the Girdle of Melian raised
Helcaraxë crossing; first Moonrise
The Sun first rises; Dagor-nuin-Giliath; death of Fëanor
Maedhros rescued from Thangorodrim
Mereth Aderthad, the Feast of Reuniting
Nargothrond founded by Finrod
Dagor Aglareb; the Siege of Angband begins
Gondolin full-wrought (52 years of toil)
Glaurung first issues from Angband — driven back by Fingon
Finrod finds the Edain in Ossiriand
Dagor Bragollach; Fingolfin falls before Angband
The Quest of the Silmaril (Beren & Lúthien)
Nirnaeth Arnoediad ('on the fourth day' the main battle)
The Havens of the Falas destroyed
Tumhalad; the Sack of Nargothrond
Deaths of Túrin and Niënor at Cabed-en-Aras
Húrin released after 28 years on Thangorodrim
Thingol slain; the Ruin of Doriath; Melian returns West
Birth of Eärendil in Gondolin
The Fall of Gondolin; Glorfindel's duel
Elrond and Elros born at the Havens of Sirion
The Third Kinslaying: the Havens of Sirion destroyed; Elwing bears the Silmaril to the sea
events.cite: WoJ, Tale of Years (dated 532→534→538 across the revisions — final layer shown)

events.label: Eärendil's embassy reaches Valinor; the Star ascends
The War of Wrath
Melkor thrust through the Door of Night
Lindon and the Grey Havens founded
The Edain reach Númenor
Death of Elros Tar-Minyatur (aged 500)
events.cite: App B; UT Line of Elros

events.label: Sauron arises again in Middle-earth
First Númenórean ship returns to Middle-earth (Entulessë)
Eregion founded
Aldarion's Guild of Venturers; the haven of Vinyalondë
events.cite: UT, Aldarion and Erendis; App B

events.label: Sauron chooses Mordor; Barad-dûr begun
Sauron comes in fair form to Eregion; Númenor begins permanent havens
The Rings of Power forged; the Three completed
The One Ring forged in Orodruin
War of the Elves and Sauron; Eregion destroyed; Imladris founded
The Nazgûl first appear
Umbar made a great fortress
Pelargir built, haven of the Faithful
Ar-Pharazôn takes Sauron to Númenor
THE DOWNFALL OF NÚMENOR; the world made round
Arnor and Gondor founded
Sauron takes Minas Ithil; Isildur escapes by ship; the Alliance is made
War of the Last Alliance; Sauron overthrown; Gil-galad and Elendil perish
Disaster of the Gladden Fields; Isildur slain; the Ring lost
Elrond weds Celebrían
The first Easterling wars; Rómendacil I falls
Arnor divided: Arthedain, Cardolan, Rhudaur
The Istari arrive over the Sea
Gondor's zenith: the Ship-kings take Umbar; Hyarmendacil's peace
The Shadow falls on Greenwood: Mirkwood; Hobbits reach Eriador
Angmar arises; the Witch-king in the North
Amon Sûl razed; Rhudaur extinguished
The Kin-strife of Gondor
The Shire granted and colonised
The Great Plague
The Wainriders destroy Gondor's northern army; Narmacil II slain
Calimehtar's victory on Dagorlad
Ondoher falls with both sons; Eärnil's Battle of the Camp
Fall of Arthedain; Battle of Fornost; Angmar broken
The Balrog awakes; Khazad-dûm abandoned; the Witch-king to Mordor
Amroth and Nimrodel lost; the Galadhrim flee the Balrog-terror
events.cite: UT, Amroth and Nimrodel; App B

events.label: Erebor founded: Thráin I comes to the Lonely Mountain
Minas Ithil falls: Minas Morgul
Eärnur lost; the Stewards rule
Gandalf goes to Dol Guldur; the Watchful Peace begins (to 2460)
The White Council is formed — and Déagol finds the Ring; Sméagol murders him
Celebrían waylaid in the Redhorn Pass; she sails West the next year
Field of Celebrant; the Oath of Eorl; Rohan founded
Battle of Greenfields: the Bullroarer slays Golfimbul
The Long Winter; Helm Hammerhand besieged in the Hornburg
Smaug descends on Erebor and Dale
Battle of Azanulbizar
Gandalf in Dol Guldur: the Necromancer is Sauron; Thráin's map and key
The Crossings of Poros: Folcwine's twin sons fall for Gondor
Bilbo born
Aragorn born
The Quest of Erebor: Smaug slain; Battle of Five Armies; the Ring found
Sauron declares himself; Barad-dûr rebuilt
The last White Council; Saruman claims Isengard
Aragorn and Arwen plight their troth on Cerin Amroth; Gollum reaches Mordor's borders
Balin's colony retakes Moria — and falls; the Chamber of Mazarbul
The Farewell Feast: Bilbo leaves the Shire
Gollum released from Mordor; taken by Aragorn; Gandalf reads Isildur's scroll
The assault on Osgiliath: the war begins; the last bridge cast down
Frodo leaves Bag End (day-level track in §3 of the document)
The Council of Elrond: 'the Ring must be destroyed'
The Fellowship of the Ring leaves Rivendell
The Bridge of Khazad-dûm: Gandalf falls with the Balrog
The Hornburg: Helm's Deep held; the Huorns; Isengard drowned by the Ents
BATTLE OF THE PELENNOR FIELDS (Gate broken at dawn; fleet at the third hour)
Battle of Dale: Brand and Dáin Ironfoot fall; Erebor besieged and freed
THE RING DESTROYED; fall of Barad-dûr
Coronation of King Elessar; Midsummer wedding of Aragorn and Arwen
Battle of Bywater; the Shire scoured
The Ring-bearers sail from the Grey Havens; the Third Age ends
Samwise sails from the Havens, last of the Ring-bearers
King Éomer dies; Merry and Pippin ride to Rohan and Gondor, and rest in Rath Dínen

The days of The New Shadow — 'One hundred and five years had passed since the fall of the Dark Tower': Tolkien's furthest-told Fourth-Age moment, the tale abandoned after a few pages (its drafts wavered between 100 and 120 years)
The Passing of King Elessar; Legolas and Gimli sail
Arwen dies at Cerin Amroth
